title: Diplazium oshimense
taxonomy: Diplazium oshimense is classified within the family Athyriaceae. This fern is a member of the genus Diplazium, which is a group of ferns commonly known as the 'wood ferns'. The genus Diplazium is further grouped into several species, including D. oshimense. It is native to Japan and belongs to the kingdom Plantae. The classification of D. oshimense is also reflected in its binomial nomenclature, which is used to identify and categorize species across different taxonomic ranks.

intro: Diplazium oshimense, also known as Oshima's maidenhair fern, is a species of vascular plant belonging to the family Athyriaceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Bioactive constituents of Diplazium oshimense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in infusions and decoctions to treat various ailments.
